notable_missing: unitree-r1 uploaded videos and contact sheets

notes: Closeout sync now mirrors the Nebius prefix with --delete while preserving only local runtime-watch files, preventing stale local evidence from satisfying production inventory.
Direct S3 listing shows no uploaded stage-10 log or checkpoint; newest run-prefix objects remain preflight-era uploads from 2026-05-22 20:02:33 UTC.

The active payload was launched before Unitree R1 was added to the generated local preflight profile set, so the remote prefix currently has 20 reviewed videos across ASIMOV-1, Hiwonder AiNex, Unitree G1, and Unitree H1, not the local 25-video all-profile set.

Local source for future launches now includes Unitree R1 in the default launch preflight and all-robot readiness/recording set, and the preflight bundle validator now fails if any default profile is omitted from 00_local_preflight.sh. evidence/full_training_preflight was regenerated at 2026-05-23T10:13:05Z with default_profiles including Unitree R1, multi_robot_readiness=true, and launch_template_hygiene=true.
Unitree R1 is now included in the learning-signal and unified-training CLI coverage alongside ASIMOV-1, Hiwonder AiNex, Unitree G1, and Unitree H1; the expanded 35-test slice passed locally.
Continual benchmark validation now requires an exact learner/seed result grid, one Alberta and one PPO result for every configured seed, with no duplicate or extra rows.

The final training comparison report now exposes joint_reach_exact_learner_seed_grid and obstacle_course_exact_learner_seed_grid completion requirements, both currently false because production benchmark artifacts have not uploaded.

Production-policy video validation now requires non-empty MP4s meeting the minimum byte threshold through a video_sizes subcheck; the current uploaded ASIMOV clips pass size checks but still fail checkpoint binding because the trained Alberta checkpoint is absent.

Multi-robot video validation now requires the manifest command list and record_combined flag to match the expected action set; the final report exposes multi_robot_video_commands_match and multi_robot_video_combined_recording_match completion requirements.

Production training-input validation now requires datasets.rl_from_sim_ready=true and datasets.offline_datasets_block_current_plan=false. The regenerated local preflight at 2026-05-23T09:49:58Z satisfies these fields and surfaces rl_from_sim_ready, imitation_training_ready, and offline_datasets_block_current_plan in preflight_report.json; the active uploaded preflight predates them, so training_inputs is now a failed closeout gate for this run.

The generated 00_local_preflight.sh now runs eliza-robot-validate-full-training-preflight evidence/full_training_preflight on the host after the local training-input, multi-robot, Brax job, and ASIMOV readiness checks.

The generated bundle now includes run_all_nebius_stages.sh via eliza-robot-run-full-training-bundle, which writes per-stage START/END logs, per-stage status JSON, runner_status.json, success/failure markers, and optional periodic logs/status uploads through NEBIUS_TRAINING_S3_URI using NEBIUS_S3_ENDPOINT; upload result summaries are recorded and status is re-uploaded afterward so future heartbeat upload failures are visible remotely.

The generated full-training preflight bundle now writes nebius_instance_launch_template.json and validates it with the launch hygiene checker. The safe template uses run_all_nebius_stages.sh, NEBIUS_TRAINING_S3_URI, NEBIUS_S3_ENDPOINT, status heartbeat evidence, and a hard shutdown cap without embedding object-storage credentials in VM metadata.

Redacted instance launch hygiene evidence was written to evidence/nebius_full_training/instance_launch_hygiene.json. The active VM fails because its older cloud-init wrapper embeds object-storage credential environment variables, bypasses the repo-owned stage runner, reconstructs upload paths instead of using NEBIUS_TRAINING_S3_URI, and has no heartbeat/status upload contract during long stages.

Launch hygiene is now a formal production validation, final-report, and artifact-inventory gate. The current closeout lists instance_launch_hygiene as missing/failed even though the redacted report is present, because the active launch used the older no-heartbeat cloud-init wrapper.

Stage status is now a formal production validation, final-report, and artifact-inventory gate. The current closeout lists stage_status as missing because the active launch predates the repo-owned bundle runner and therefore has no status/runner_status.json or status/<stage>.json heartbeat artifacts in object storage.
Backend comparison generation now validates untrained, Alberta, and PPO eval outputs before writing artifacts, rejecting missing, NaN/Inf, or boolean reward evidence.
Cleanup remains blocked until closeout, finalization, artifact inventory, production validation, and the final training-comparison report are all green.

Relaunch readiness is now tracked by eliza-robot-plan-nebius-training-relaunch. The current relaunch_plan blocks a duplicate H200 run because the active run is still running before hard cap, while confirming the regenerated preflight bundle and safe launch template are ready.

The strict Alberta objective audit now writes evidence/alberta_objective_completion_audit.json and .md. Latest audit passes alberta_framework_integrated and clean_relaunch_path_ready, but fails six production-dependent requirements until Nebius training artifacts arrive.

The hard-cap-exceeded stale H200 instance was stopped after no new object-storage artifacts appeared beyond preflight; a redacted state report is in evidence/nebius_full_training/instance_state_redacted.json.

Relaunch readiness is now green because the active stale run exceeded its hard cap and the regenerated safe preflight/launch template validates; a clean production run still needs to be launched to satisfy the objective audit.

A clean production relaunch payload was packaged and uploaded to Object Storage under robot-full-clean-1779556360 with a launch-hygiene-clean instance spec; compute creation is waiting on Nebius CLI OAuth re-authentication.

The clean launch resume command is implemented as eliza-robot-launch-prepared-nebius-clean-run and was attempted; it blocks at Nebius disk creation until CLI OAuth browser authentication is refreshed.

The clean relaunch payload was reduced from 1.8 GB to about 57 MB by excluding local environments, caches, stale Nebius sync artifacts, checkpoints, and large vendor/demo assets while preserving full-training preflight and video evidence.

The clean launch command now performs a Nebius CLI auth preflight before reading runtime secrets or creating compute resources. The latest resume attempt exited quickly with state awaiting_nebius_cli_auth, no disk or instance IDs, and redacted auth evidence instead of hanging in disk creation.

Production artifact inventory now requires evidence/ALBERTA_END_TO_END_REPORT.json and .md, and the generated 50_post_train_validation.sh stage now runs eliza-robot-generate-alberta-report after checkpoint-bound video review so future Nebius runs archive the detailed Alberta/PPO/obstacle/video evidence bundle.

The Alberta end-to-end report generator now accepts explicit production evidence paths. Future Nebius post-training validation writes backend and continual benchmark validation_report.json files, then generates evidence/ALBERTA_END_TO_END_REPORT.json from production backend_compare/asimov-1, alberta_obstacle_course, and checkpoint-bound video review artifacts instead of hardcoded smoke paths.

record_agent_videos now preserves existing all-robot video manifest entries even during checkpoint-bound production recording. The trained target profile remains policy-checkpoint-bound, while other profiles are marked as existing_files, so human review and multi-robot validation retain the full robot set after post-training video generation.

The clean relaunch payload was refreshed and re-uploaded at 2026-05-23T17:56:01Z; the current archive is 73,934,674 bytes with SHA256 4008a9a7f365e2f0f940c5688ca833c24efd1babb6407a37b4da99bf9f6832c6, superseding the earlier 57 MB optimized archive. It includes the production-aware Alberta end-to-end report generator, saved backend/continual validation reports, manifest-preserving checkpoint video recording, and required Alberta end-to-end report artifacts.

A refreshed clean-launch resume attempt at 2026-05-23T17:58:02Z exited at the Nebius auth preflight after 20 seconds with nebius_cli_auth_required; no disk, instance, public IP, SSH readiness, or runtime secret injection occurred.

Generated the missing synced-run evidence/ALBERTA_END_TO_END_REPORT.json beside the existing Markdown report. The report is intentionally ok=false and production_complete=false because the old synced run has no production backend comparison or obstacle-course benchmark artifacts; artifact inventory now has all review-report artifacts present but still 35 production artifacts missing.

The Alberta end-to-end report and Nebius training-comparison report now distinguish internally consistent evidence from claim support. Local smoke evidence is video-consistent across 25 videos and supports the obstacle-course Alberta advantage, but its tiny robot-backend smoke comparison is currently PPO by mean reward; production claim support remains false until the clean Nebius run completes with checkpoints, backend comparison, continual benchmarks, and checkpoint-bound videos.

The clean relaunch payload was rebuilt and re-uploaded again at 2026-05-23T18:06:59Z after tightening Alberta report claim-support gates. The current archive is 168,579,109 bytes with SHA256 e2e5864a13159181db01b89b5604b6b6485c89e0c14b21e38cb14b4a098ced40 and excludes local environments, Nebius sync artifacts, ASIMOV mechanical STEP payloads, nested .git directories, and large Unitree simulation build/demo assets.

After the stricter-report payload upload, a clean-launch resume attempt at 2026-05-23T18:07:44Z again exited at the Nebius auth preflight with nebius_cli_auth_required; no disk, instance, public IP, SSH readiness, or runtime secret injection occurred.

Ran a stronger local ASIMOV-1 profile-backend Alberta-vs-PPO comparison at 4,000 steps over stand_up, walk_forward, turn_left, and turn_right. The evaluation has meaningful rollout depth (minimum mean steps survived 49), but PPO wins by mean reward by 87.461383, so local robot-backend Alberta advantage remains unsupported. The local obstacle-course continual benchmark still supports Alberta over PPO with ACC delta 1.596051 and forgetting delta 0.0.
Backend comparison validation now records survival metrics and the generated production preflight requires --min-eval-mean-steps 20 so future production completion cannot pass with one-step rollouts.
